data.title: BRIXMOR PROPERTY GROUP ANNOUNCES TAX REPORTING INFORMATION FOR 2025 DISTRIBUTIONS
data.description:
NEW YORK, Jan. 20, 2026 /PRNewswire/ — Brixmor Property Group Inc. ("Brixmor" or the "Company") announced today the tax reporting information for the 2025 distributions on its common stock. The tax reporting information as it will be reported on the Form 1099-DIV, on a per share basis, is as follows:

COMMON SHARES ; CUSIP # 11120U105

 
                                               Total Capital                          Section   Section 
                         Total        Total        Gain       Unrecaptured  Return     199A       897 
 Record     Payable   Distribution  Ordinary   Distributions    Sec.1250      of     Dividends  Capital 
   Date       Date     Per Share    Dividends       (1)         Gain (2)    Capital     (3)      Gain 
---------  ---------  ------------  ---------  -------------  ------------  -------  ---------  ------- 
 1/03/25    1/15/25     $0.2875      $0.2804      $0.0071       $0.0071     $0.0000   $0.2804   $0.0000 
---------  ---------  ------------  ---------  -------------  ------------  -------  ---------  ------- 
 4/02/25    4/15/25     $0.2875      $0.2804      $0.0071       $0.0071     $0.0000   $0.2804   $0.0000 
---------  ---------  ------------  ---------  -------------  ------------  -------  ---------  ------- 
 7/02/25    7/15/25     $0.2875      $0.2804      $0.0071       $0.0071     $0.0000   $0.2804   $0.0000 
---------  ---------  ------------  ---------  -------------  ------------  -------  ---------  ------- 
10/02/25   10/15/25     $0.2875      $0.2804      $0.0071       $0.0071     $0.0000   $0.2804   $0.0000 
---------  ---------  ------------  ---------  -------------  ------------  -------  ---------  ------- 
       Totals           $1.1500      $1.1216      $0.0284       $0.0284     $0.0000   $1.1216   $0.0000 
 -------------------  ------------  ---------  -------------  ------------  -------  ---------  ------- 
 
 
(1)  Pursuant to Treas. Reg. -- 1.1061-6(c), the Company reports that for 
     purposes of section 1061 of the Internal Revenue Code, the One Year 
     Amounts Disclosure and the Three Year Amounts Disclosure are $0.00 with 
     respect to direct and indirect holders of "applicable partnership 
     interests." 
(2)  Represents additional characterization of amounts included in Total 
     Capital Gain Distributions. 
(3)  Represents additional characterization of amounts included in Total 
     Ordinary Dividends.

CONNECT WITH BRIXMOR

  • For additional information, please visit https://www.brixmor.com;
  • Follow Brixmor on:
    -- LinkedIn at https://www.linkedin.com/company/brixmor

    -- Facebook at https://www.facebook.com/Brixmor

    -- Instagram at https://www.instagram.com/brixmorpropertygroup; and

    -- YouTube at https://www.youtube.com/user/Brixmor.

ABOUT BRIXMOR PROPERTY GROUP

Brixmor is a real estate investment trust (REIT) that owns and operates a high-quality, national portfolio of open-air shopping centers. Its 354 retail centers comprise approximately 63 million square feet of prime retail space in established trade areas. The Company strives to own and operate shopping centers that reflect Brixmor's vision "to be the center of the communities we serve" and are home to a diverse mix of thriving national, regional and local retailers. Brixmor is a proud real estate partner to over 5,000 retailers including The TJX Companies, The Kroger Co., Publix Super Markets and Ross Stores.

Brixmor announces material information to its investors in SEC filings and press releases and on public conference calls, webcasts and the "Investors" page of its website at https://www.brixmor.com. The Company also uses social media to communicate with its investors and the public, and the information Brixmor posts on social media may be deemed material information. Therefore, Brixmor encourages investors and others interested in the Company to review the information that it posts on its website and on its social media channels.

SAFE HARBOR LANGUAGE

This press release may contain forward-looking statements within the meaning of Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933 and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. These statements include, but are not limited to, statements related to our expectations regarding the performance of our business, our financial results, our liquidity and capital resources, and other non-historical statements. You can identify these forward-looking statements by the use of words such as "outlook," "believes," "expects," "potential," "continues," "may," "will," "should, " "seeks," "projects," "predicts," "intends," "plans," "estimates," "anticipates," or the negative version of these words or other comparable words. Such forward-looking statements are subject to various risks and uncertainties. Accordingly, there are or will be important factors that could cause actual outcomes or results to differ materially from those indicated in these statements. We believe these factors include, but are not limited to, those described under the sections entitled "Forward-Looking Statements" and "Risk Factors" in our Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024, as such factors may be updated from time to time in our periodic filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC"), which are accessible on the SEC's website at https://www.sec.gov. These factors include (1) changes in national, regional, and local economies, due to global events such as international military conflicts, international trade disputes, a foreign debt crisis, foreign currency volatility, or due to domestic issues, such as government policies and regulations, tariffs, energy prices, market dynamics, general economic contractions, rising interest rates, inflation, unemployment, or limited growth in consumer income or spending; (2) local real estate market conditions, including an oversupply of space in, or a reduction in demand for, properties similar to those in our Portfolio (defined hereafter); (3) competition from other available properties and e-commerce; (4) disruption and/or consolidation in the retail sector, the financial stability of our tenants, and the overall financial condition of large retailing companies, including their ability to pay rent and/or expense reimbursements that are due to us; (5) in the case of percentage rents, the sales volumes of our tenants; (6) increases in property operating expenses, including common area expenses, utilities, insurance, and real estate taxes, which are relatively inflexible and generally do not decrease if revenue or occupancy decrease; (7) increases in the costs to repair, renovate, and re-lease space; (8) earthquakes, wildfires, tornadoes, hurricanes, damage from rising sea levels due to climate change, other natural disasters, epidemics and/or pandemics, civil unrest, terrorist acts, or acts of war, any of which may result in uninsured or underinsured losses; and (9) changes in laws and governmental regulations, including those governing usage, zoning, the environment, privacy, data security, intellectual property rights, and taxes. These factors should not be construed as exhaustive and should be read in conjunction with the other cautionary statements that are included in this press release and in our periodic filings. The forward-looking statements speak only as of the date of this press release, and we expressly disclaim any obligation or undertaking to publicly update or review any forward-looking statement, whether as a result of new information, future developments, or otherwise, except to the extent otherwise required by law.

data.title: Clearmind Medicine: Net loss narrowed, but ongoing losses, no revenue, and funding needs raise going concern risk
data.description:
Net loss narrowed to $3.86M in FY2025 as R&D spending increased and G&A costs fell, but the company remains pre-revenue and faces substantial going concern risk. Ongoing clinical trials for MEAI in AUD and obesity/metabolic disorders are progressing, with continued reliance on external financing and exposure to regulatory and geopolitical risks.

data.title: Treasury market has worst day in months after Trump threatens European allies with tariffs related to Greenland
data.description:
By Vivien Lou Chen

The yield on the 30-year U.S. government bond briefly headed closer to 5% on Tuesday, in a sign of worry about the impact of tariffs on inflation

U.S. government bonds were taking a beating after President Donald Trump's latest tariff threat against Europe.

Long-dated U.S. government debt was getting slammed on Tuesday as nervous investors reacted to President Donald Trump's plans to slap 10% tariffs on imports from eight European countries beginning next month.

Tuesday's selloff within the $30 trillion Treasury market sent the benchmark 10-year yield BX:TMUBMUSD10Y up by as much as 8.1 basis points to an intraday high of 4.31%, leaving it poised for what might be its highest closing level in five months. The rate on the 30-year bond BX:TMUBMUSD30Y jumped by as much as 11.2 basis points to as high as 4.95% and was briefly headed for a closing level not seen in more than four months.

At one point, the long bond seemed poised for its biggest one-day selloff since July 11, when it was rocked by renewed fears about U.S. trade wars with Canada and Europe. Yields and bond prices move in opposite directions, meaning that yields spike during aggressive selloffs of the corresponding Treasury maturity.

Investors used the bond market on Tuesday to express their worries about global geopolitical uncertainties stretching from the U.S. to Europe and Japan. Those fears centered on the risks of a continued selloff in U.S. assets and of a broad-based bond-market crisis that drew comparisons to the one that unfolded in the U.K. more than three years ago.

Europe ranks as one of the biggest holders of U.S. government debt. The euro area combined with Belgium, Luxembourg and Ireland held a total of more than $1.5 trillion in Treasurys as of last year. And European-based market participants were not taking kindly to Trump's latest trade move.

Danish pension fund AkademikerPension, for example, was reportedly set to divest its holdings in the U.S. government bond market by the end of the month. "The U.S. is basically not a good credit and long-term the U.S. government finances are not sustainable," Anders Schelde, chief investment officer at AkademikerPension, told Bloomberg on Tuesday.

By quickly breaking through trading ranges that have prevailed for months, 10- and 30-year Treasury yields - which are used as benchmarks for the cost of borrowing on everything from mortgages and consumer loans to corporate bonds - signaled that broader risks may lie ahead. This was the case even though the bond-market selloff moderated in afternoon trading, leaving the 10-year yield up by 4.3 basis points at 4.27% and the 30-year yield higher by 5.7 basis points at 4.9%.

The 10-year yield jumped past 4.25% on Tuesday partly because of "rising rumors that Europeans could 'weaponize their U.S. assets' - yes, weaponize is the word being used - to retaliate against Mr. Trump's aggressive trade and geopolitical policies," Ipek Ozkardeskaya, a senior analyst at Swiss-based digital bank and online trading platform Swissquote, said in a note.

Europeans hold roughly $10 trillion in total U.S. assets, around $6 trillion of which is in American equities, Ozkardeskaya said. "Selling those assets would pull the rug from under U.S. markets" and could possibly get Trump's attention. However, it would also force European investors, private and public, to "willingly accepting financial pain to punish the U.S." - something that is hard to imagine at this point.

Tom Nakamura, head of fixed income and currencies at AGF Investments in Toronto, said the U.S. Treasury market tends to reflect "a lot of different views. In times of stress or global concerns, it can act as a lightning rod for the expression of those factors."

In his view, Tuesday's bond-market selloff was being driven by two main things. The first has to do with concerns about Trump's desire to acquire Greenland from Denmark, which sparked the president's latest threat of tariffs against Europe. These trade concerns were producing near-term worry about economic growth and raising the possible need for easing by central banks, while also leading to long-term fears about inflation and fiscal risks, he said in a phone interview. This was creating what's known as a steepening of the Treasury curve, a dynamic in which the difference between short-term and long-term yields widens. The second catalyst for Tuesday's moves was Japan, with investors keeping an eye on whether Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi will be able to deliver a large boost for the economy.

Also read: Japan's long-bond yields surge to record highs. Why it may be a problem beyond Tokyo.On Tuesday, Japan's 40-year bond yield BX:TMBMKJP-40Y surged to 4.215%, its highest level since its introduction in 2007. The long-dated bond was continuing to "bleed" and was creating a "shock" that was transmitting across global rates markets, according to Pooja Kumra, a senior European and U.K. rates strategist for TD Securities. "De-risking and margin calls remain material risks to trigger broader market repercussions," she said - similar to the severe market disruptions seen during the 2022 U.K. gilt crisis, which was triggered by the British government's unfunded tax-cut plans.

It will take a lot more than Tuesday's moves in the U.S. bond market, however, to create more long-term doubts about its attractiveness to investors.

The U.S. bond market "remains the largest and most liquid" in the world and "is central to a lot of financial-system plumbing," said Nakamura of AGF Investments. "While we can have these concerns on the margins, in terms of that role being diminished materially, it is likely to be a long process. We have a long time before we get concerned about a protracted selloff in Treasurys."

-Vivien Lou Chen

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Clear Street, Cloud-Native Capital Markets Platform for Trading, Clearing, Custody, and Financing, Files for Nasdaq IPO
data.description:
Clear Street filed for an initial public offering and plans to list on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker “CSIG.” The New York-based fintech provides a cloud-native platform for trading, clearing, custody, risk management, and financing across multiple asset classes. The company did not disclose a price range or share count yet and said proceeds will fund working capital, capital expenditures, and general corporate purposes.

Business Description

Clear Street is a financial infrastructure technology company that operates a cloud-native, end-to-end capital markets platform built on a single real-time ledger. Its software unifies trading, risk and margin, clearing and custody, lifecycle management, and financing and stock loan across equities, options, futures, security-based swaps, foreign exchange, fixed income, and select digital assets. Clients access the platform via Studio, a unified web and API interface that supports execution management, portfolio analytics, and regulatory and operational workflows.

The company earns revenue primarily from net financing (customer margin and securities lending, collateralized financing, and related funding activity) and transaction revenues (commissions, clearing, locate fees, investment banking, and pass-through fees). Competitive advantages include a client-centric architecture with real-time visibility, speed to launch new applications and asset classes, and elastic scale and reliability on the cloud. Clear Street positions itself as a modern alternative to legacy, fragmented systems, serving sophisticated investors who need institutional-grade infrastructure without duplicating technology stacks.

Market Overview

  • Total addressable market: The company estimates a $310 billion TAM, including a $128 billion serviceable market in its current scope.
  • Market growth: Rising trading volumes, 24/6 and 24/7 market access, and expanding digital asset activity are accelerating infrastructure demand.
  • Market position: Clear Street targets sophisticated investors underserved by legacy platforms, aiming to unify trading, risk, and financing on one real-time ledger.
  • Key competitors: Large banks/custodians, tech-led retail and professional platforms, and point-solution vendors.
  • Industry trends: Shift to cloud-native infrastructure, real-time risk, shorter settlement cycles (T+1), global participation, and growing data/AI-driven workflows.

Operational Metrics

  • Customers: Over 2,000 clients as of September 2025.
  • Daily trading activity: More than $31.3 billion in September 2025.
  • Balances: $17.2 billion in interest-bearing client balances as of September 30, 2025.
  • Market share: Approximately 3.8% of U.S. equity market clearing as of September 30, 2025.
  • Transaction volumes: Over $4 trillion traded in 2024; more than $5 trillion in the first nine months of 2025.
  • Transactions: About 50 million transactions per month in 2024; approximately 90 million transactions in September 2025.
  • Client retention: 99.3% retention among Institutions and Intermediaries in 2024.
  • Client expansion: Institutional clients engaged in 3+ activities grew 81% from 48 to 87 through September 30, 2025.
  • Revenue concentration: 37 clients generated at least $1 million in 2024; 90 clients (annualized) in the first nine months of 2025.
  • End-user reach: Clients directly or indirectly serve more than 40 million end-users.
  • Exchanges & memberships: Members of 52 exchanges and clearing houses as of September 30, 2025.
  • Geographic presence: Live in the U.S., Canada, and the U.K.; licensing efforts in the EU and Asia ongoing.

Financials Highlights

  • Revenue (current): Nine months ended Sept. 30, 2025: $783.7 million; FY2024: $463.6 million
  • Revenue growth: 160% year-over-year for the nine months ended Sept. 30, 2025; 137% year-over-year for FY2024
  • Operating income: FY2024: $91.7 million; Nine months ended Sept. 30, 2025: $324.6 million
  • Net income: FY2024: $89.1 million; Nine months ended Sept. 30, 2025: $157.2 million

Management

  • Edward T. Tilly, Chief Executive Officer - Former Chairman and CEO of Cboe Global Markets; joined Clear Street in 2024 and became CEO in 2026.
  • Steven Bisgay, Chief Financial Officer - Former CFO of Cantor Fitzgerald and BGC Partners; prior senior roles at KCG and Knight Capital.
  • Jonathan Daplyn, Chief Operating Officer - Joined from Morgan Stanley where he led Prime Brokerage and Derivatives Clearing Technology.
  • John Levene, Chief Experience Officer - Spent 27 years at Goldman Sachs; led Global Banking and Markets Client Experience.
  • Uriel Cohen, Executive Chairman - Co-founder; previously co-founded Alpine Global Management and has extensive capital markets experience.
  • Elli Ausubel, Executive Vice Chairman - Senior strategy leader and long-time investor; focuses on strategic growth and partnerships.

IPO Structure

  • Issuer: Clear Street Group Inc.
  • Filing date: January 20, 2026
  • Proposed ticker: CSIG
  • Exchange: Nasdaq Global Select Market
  • Lead underwriters: Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC; BofA Securities; Morgan Stanley; UBS Investment Bank; Clear Street
  • Use of proceeds: Working capital, capital expenditures, and general corporate purposes.

Original SEC Filing: Clear Street Group Inc. - S-1 - Jan. 20, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Clear Street files for US IPO
data.description:
Wall Street brokerage firm Clear Street Group filed for an U.S. initial public offering on Tuesday.

The U.S. IPO market is primed to continue its long-awaited rebound in 2026, with analysts expecting a potentially busy start as companies that delayed listings during last year's historic government shutdown could provide early momentum.

Clear Street will list on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol "CSIG".

Goldman Sachs, BofA Securities, Morgan Stanley and UBS Investment Bank are among the underwriters for the offering.

data.title: Netflix Looks to AI to Grow Ad Business — Market Talk
data.description: Netflix is doubling down on artificial intelligence to boost its advertising business. Last year, the streaming platform began testing new AI tools to help advertisers create custom ads based on its intellectual property, and plans to continue that work this year, the company said in a letter to shareholders. Netflix has also used AI to automate workflows for ad concepts and streamline campaign planning, speeding up both processes, it said. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

As noted above, the common stock dividend paid on January 15, 2025, with a record date of December 31, 2024, has been allocated entirely to 2025. The common stock dividend of $0.19 per share that was paid on January 15, 2026, with a record date of December 31, 2025, will be allocated entirely to 2026.

About Douglas Emmett, Inc.

Douglas Emmett, Inc. (DEI) is a fully integrated, self-administered and self-managed real estate investment trust (REIT), and one of the largest owners and operators of high-quality office and multifamily properties located in the premier coastal submarkets of Los Angeles and Honolulu. Douglas Emmett focuses on owning and acquiring a substantial share of top-tier office properties and premier multifamily communities in neighborhoods that possess significant supply constraints, high-end executive housing and key lifestyle amenities. For more information about Douglas Emmett, please visit our website at www.douglasemmett.com.

data.title: Ibovespa Sets Record High
data.description:
Brazil’s Ibovespa rose 0.9% to close at a fresh record of 166,277, supported by strength in heavyweight stocks that offset a softer global tone and lingering trade uncertainty.

Commodity linked leaders underpinned the advance, with Petrobras firming 0.7% alongside higher oil prices and Vale 2% gaining on resilient iron ore pricing, helping anchor the index despite pressure in more rate sensitive and consumer facing names.

Financials also contributed as stable domestic conditions and an orderly local rate curve sustained demand for large banks.

In the meantime, Sabesp jumped 3.6% after regulatory approval for its acquisition of Emae, reinforcing confidence in corporate activity.

While renewed tariff threats from the US and broader risk aversion tempered appetite for cyclicals, steady inflation expectations and a still supportive macro backdrop at home limited downside pressure.

data.title: Interactive Brokers Group 4Q Profit, Sales Rise on Higher Trading Volumes
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Interactive Brokers Group logged higher profit and sales in its latest quarter as trading volume rose across stocks, options and futures.

The online brokerage platform on Tuesday posted a fourth-quarter profit of $284 million, or 63 cents a share, compared with $217 million, or 50 cents a share, a year earlier.

Adjusted earnings per share were 65 cents, ahead of estimates of 59 cents a share, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

Revenue rose to $1.64 billion from $1.39 billion, in line with analyst projections.

Commission revenue rose 22% to $582 million on higher customer trading volumes. Trading volumes in options, futures and stocks increased 27%, 22% and 16%, respectively.

Customer accounts increased by 32% to 4.4 million, with customer equity up 37% to $779.9 billion.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Zions Bancorporation Reports Strong Q4 2025 Financial Results
data.description:
Zions Bancorporation, N.A., a prominent financial services company operating in 11 western states, has released its fourth-quarter 2025 financial results. The company reported significant growth in key financial metrics, reflecting robust performance and strategic initiatives aimed at enhancing shareholder value.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2025, Zions Bancorporation reported net earnings of $262 million, translating to a diluted earnings per share (EPS) of $1.76. This marks a notable increase from the $200 million in net earnings and $1.34 diluted EPS reported in the same quarter of the previous year. The annual net earnings for 2025 stood at $895 million, with a diluted EPS of $6.01, compared to $737 million and $4.95, respectively, in 2024.

Net interest income for the quarter was $683 million, up 9% from the previous year, driven by lower funding costs and a favorable shift in the composition of average interest-earning assets. The net interest margin improved to 3.31%, compared to 3.05% in the prior year period.

Business and Operational Highlights

Pre-provision net revenue (PPNR) for the quarter was $356 million, a 10% increase from the previous year. Adjusted PPNR was $331 million, up 6%. Customer-related noninterest income rose by 1% to $177 million, while noninterest expense increased by 7% to $546 million. Adjusted noninterest expense was $548 million, up 8%, primarily due to a $15 million charitable contribution during the quarter.

Loans and leases grew by 3% to $60.9 billion, with a significant reduction in the annualized ratio of net loan and lease charge-offs to average loans and leases, which dropped to 0.05% from 0.24%. The provision for credit losses was $6 million, down from $41 million in the previous year.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

Zions Bancorporation's capital position strengthened, with the estimated Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) capital ratio rising to 11.5% from 10.9%. Tangible book value per common share increased by 21% to $40.79. The company also reversed an FDIC Special Assessment accrual of $9 million, contributing positively to the financial results.

In terms of deposits, total deposits were $75.6 billion, down 1% from the previous year, while customer deposits (excluding brokered deposits) increased by 1% to $71.8 billion. Short-term borrowings decreased by 19% to $3.1 billion.

Management's Perspective

Harris H. Simmons, Chairman and CEO of Zions Bancorporation, expressed satisfaction with the fourth-quarter results, highlighting a 31% increase in earnings per share compared to the previous year. He noted the positive operating leverage achieved during the quarter and the strong credit quality, with annualized net charge-offs totaling 0.05% of loans. Simmons also emphasized the company's strengthened capital position and the continued growth in loans and deposits.

Future Outlook

Looking ahead, Zions Bancorporation anticipates continued prudent growth in 2026. The company aims to build on the strong performance of 2025, with a focus on maintaining robust credit quality, enhancing capital strength, and achieving positive operating leverage.

Original SEC Filing: ZIONS BANCORPORATION, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION /UT/ [ ZION ] - 8-K - Jan. 20, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: United Airlines offers upbeat outlook on strong premium travel demand
data.description:
United Airlines on Tuesday issued an upbeat outlook for the current quarter and the full year, buoyed by strong demand from higher‑income and corporate travelers.

The Chicago‑based carrier also exceeded profit expectations for the December quarter.

United forecast first-quarter adjusted profit of $1 to $1.50 per share. The midpoint of the range, $1.25, is above analysts' average estimate of $1.13, according to LSEG data.

For 2026, United projected adjusted earnings of $12 to $14 per share, compared with analysts' average estimate of $13.16 a share.

Its adjusted earnings for the fourth quarter came in at $3.10 per share, topping analysts' estimate of $2.94. Total revenue rose 4.8% to $15.4 billion.

data.title: US stocks battered amid building uncertainty
data.description:
Key points:
  • Main US indexes end sharply lower; Nasdaq off most, down ~2.4%
  • Tech, Cons Disc weakest S&P 500 sectors; Staples sole gainer
  • Dollar tumbles; bitcoin down >3.5%; crude, gold both up >1.5%
  • US 10-Year Treasury yield jumps to ~4.29%

US STOCKS BATTERED AMID BUILDING UNCERTAINTY

The main U.S. stock indexes tumbled on Tuesday, as investors returned from the U.S. long weekend and reacted negatively to fresh tariff threats from President Donald Trump against Europe and renewed volatility in global markets.

The risk-off wave helped vault gold to fresh record highs, knocked stocks lower globally and left U.S. Treasuries wobbling under renewed selling pressure.

Indeed, the PHLX Gold/Silver Index (.XAU) was able to score yet another record close on Tuesday. This, while the Dow slid more than 1.7% and the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite both tumbled more than 2% each.

The U.S. 10-Year Treasury yield hit 4.313%, or its highest level since August 22 of last year. The yield rise was more pronounced on the long end of the curve, as investors not only reacted to President Trump's renewed threat of a trade clash with Europe over its proposed acquisition of Greenland, but also to turbulence in Japanese government bonds.

With the day's instability, the Nasdaq finished below its 50-day moving average (DMA) for the first time since January 2 of this year, while the S&P 500 ended below its 50-DMA for the first time since December 17.

Nearly all S&P 500 sectors ended red with Tech and Consumer Discretionary taking the biggest hits. Just defensive Staples edged into the green at close.

All seven members of the Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF ended down on the day. The ETF lost 3% for its biggest one-day drop since October 30. However, in another pocket of some strength, the Nasdaq Biotech Index ended modestly higher.

In any event, despite the day's carnage, recent relative performance trends persisted. Value held up better than Growth (.IGX), and the S&P 500 Equal Weighted Index (.SPXEW) fell less than the cap-weighted index.

Although the small-cap Russell 2000 lost 1.2%, it also held up better than the SPX. Tuesday marked the 12th day in a row that this small-cap index has outperformed the S&P 500. The last streak of small-cap outperformance that was longer was a 13-day run that ended in June 2008.

Meanwhile, amid bubbling uncertainty, the CBOE Volatility Index is on pace to end above 20.00 for the first time since November 24.

Here is a snapshot of where markets stood just shortly after 4 p.m. ET.
